TITLE OF RESEARCH: Online Booking and Scheduling Management System for
Professional Event Entertainers Service

John Michael P. Soguilon

APPROVAL SHEET
This capstone project titled Online Booking and Scheduling Management System for
Professional Event Entertainers Service prepared and submitted by Gabriel Nicole C.
Benedicto, EJ Ridge L. Lerios, John Michael P. Soguilon, and Daniel B. Tuprio in partial
fulfillment of the requirements for the degree of Bachelor of Science in Information Technology,
has been examined and is recommended for acceptance and approval.
Mr. Richard A. Santos
Capstone Project Adviser
TABLE OF CONTENTS
Title Page I
Endorsement Form for Oral Defense II
Approval Sheet III
Table of Contents IV
INTRODUCTION 1
Project Context 1
Purpose and Description 2
INTRODUCTION
Project Context
In the Philippines, event-based services such as magic shows, puppet shows, bubble shows, and
party hosting are often managed through manual methods, including phone calls, social media
messages, handwritten schedules, and personal messaging applications. While these practices are
commonly used, they often result in operational issues such as double booking, schedule conflicts,
delayed confirmations, missed appointments, inaccurate event details, and poor record
management. These problems become more significant as the number of clients and bookings
In response to these concerns, there is a clear need for a computerized booking and scheduling
management system that can provide a more organized, accurate, and efficient way of handling
event service operations. These conditions highlight the need for a reliable booking and scheduling
management system that can help professional entertainers and event service providers manage
appointments, prevent double bookings, maintain organized records, and improve overall service
efficiency.
Purpose and Description
The purpose of this project is to design and develop an organized online booking and scheduling
improves accessibility, organization, and efficiency compared to traditional manual booking
methods.
The project is also connected to the second objective, which focuses on designing a reporting and
analytics module that generates and displays overall sales and booking data, including daily,
weekly, monthly, and yearly sales reports, while supporting the identification of package trends.
Since all booking requests, payment submissions, package selections, and transaction records are
processed and stored within the system, the reports module can provide organized data for
monitoring, review, and decision-making. Through this module, the Owner can monitor income,
To design and develop an online booking and scheduling management system for professional
entertainer services that automates client booking, service selection, scheduling, payment handling,
refund processing, and event management to improve the efficiency, accuracy, organization, and service
quality of the business.
SPECIFIC OBJECTIVES
The project specifically aims to:
• To design an online booking module that allows clients to select services, event details,
date, time, location, and packages through a digital platform.
The proponents addressed this objective by designing a dedicated reports module
that centralizes booking, payment, and transaction records for business monitoring
and review. Since all booking requests, confirmed reservations, payment
submissions, and package selections are processed and stored within the system,
these records can serve as the basis for generating sales and booking reports.
The reports module allows the Owner to view daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly
sales summaries, monitor booking performance, review income-related records,
and identify frequently selected service packages. Through this module, the system
and monitored through the calendar. Through this role-based structure and
scheduling process, the system supports more organized reservation management
and helps minimize overlapping bookings.
Scope and Limitations
Scope
The proposed Online Booking and Scheduling Management System for Professional Event
Entertainers Service covers the major operations needed to manage client bookings, service
packages, schedules, payments, refund requests, reschedule requests, reports, notifications, and
• Homepage module – The Homepage Module presents the public landing page of Aries
Magic. It displays the business information, available entertainment services, highlighted
packages, and call-to-action buttons for booking or viewing packages. This module helps
visitors understand the services offered before creating an account or submitting a
booking request.
• Booking Module - The Booking Module allows clients to submit a formal booking request
through the system. In this module, clients can select their preferred service package and
provide the required event information such as event date, event time, event type, venue,
submit it for Owner review. The Owner will then approve or reject the refund request
based on booking and payment records.
• Reschedule Module – The Reschedule Request Module allows clients to request changes
to their event schedule. Clients can select an existing booking, propose a new date and
time, and submit the request for review. The request will be reviewed by the Owner based
on schedule availability.
• Notifications Module – The Notifications Module keeps clients updated about important
booking activities. Clients receive notifications regarding booking status, payment
the booking information, payment history, reason for refund, and refund eligibility before
approving or rejecting the request.
• Calendar Management Module - The Calendar Management Module allows the Owner
to monitor and control confirmed bookings, blocked dates, booking limits, and event
schedules. This module helps prevent overbooking and schedule conflicts by providing a
centralized view of event availability and confirmed reservations.
• History Module - The History Module allows the Owner to view completed events,
archived bookings, transaction history, and important activity records. This module helps
• Admin Dashboard Module - The Admin Dashboard Module provides an overview of
system activities and operational records. It allows the admin to monitor booking counts,
pending requests, service records, user accounts, and important system activities.
• Booking Management Module – The Booking Management Module allows the admin to
monitor and manage booking records submitted by clients. The admin can view booking
details, search bookings, filter records, check important event information, and approve
or reject initial booking requests before clients proceed to payment submission. This
module helps keep booking records organized and accessible.
Staff Module
The Staff Module supports the Owner and Admin in daily booking and scheduling operations.
Staff members have limited access compared to the Owner and Admin. Their main responsibility
is to monitor records, check schedules, assist in service-related tasks, review completed events,
and support operational coordination.
• Staff Dashboard Module - The Staff Dashboard Module provides staff with a quick
overview of operational tasks and booking activities. It helps staff monitor pending
bookings, upcoming events, completed events, and important notifications.
Limitations
• Limited payment mode – The system does not support direct integration with online
payment APIs; processing is limited to manual verification of uploads.
• Internet dependency – The system requires an internet or local server connection to
function.
• Exclusively Designed – The system is exclusively designed for the business operations of
Aries the Magic Artist, focusing on the specific booking and management requirements of
the owner and is not intended for use by other entertainment entities.
Fontanilla et al. (2025) explored the use of online reservation systems in restaurants and found that
digital reservation platforms improve service efficiency by organizing customer bookings and
reducing waiting time. However, the researchers also noted that improper management of
reservation systems may still lead to scheduling errors such as double bookings. This study is
relevant to the proposed system because scheduling conflicts and double bookings are also
common problems experienced by event entertainers who rely on manual booking methods. The
findings of this research support the need for an organized reservation management system. In the
proposed system, this concept will be implemented through automatic schedule management, real-
proposed system because it highlights the importance of organized entertainment services within
digital platforms. Similar to tourism services, event entertainment also requires proper
organization of service offerings. In the proposed system, this concept is implemented by
organizing entertainer services into structured service packages such as magic shows, puppet
shows, bubble shows, and party hosting. This allows clients to easily browse and select the most
appropriate entertainment service for their event.

Lopez et al. (2023) emphasized that technology integration and user-friendly interfaces
significantly influence customer satisfaction in hospitality and entertainment services. The study
found that transparent information and smooth navigation improve user trust and service
experience. This research is relevant to the proposed system because it highlights the importance
of clear system design and service information. The idea will be implemented by presenting service
descriptions, packages, and booking details in a clear and organized interface, allowing clients to
understand available entertainment services before making a booking.
“Ticketing and Reservation Patterns in Entertainment Events”
Gaspay et al. (2025) explained that digital event management systems automate planning processes
such as booking coordination, itinerary generation, and event scheduling. Automation reduces
manual tasks and improves efficiency in event-related operations. This literature is directly related
to the proposed system because the system also aims to automate event booking and scheduling
processes. In the proposed platform, automation will be implemented through automatic booking
confirmations, event schedule generation, and centralized client data storage, reducing the need
for manual coordination.

goal of improving booking accuracy, scheduling organization, and service clarity to enhance

“Evolution of Modern Reservation Systems as Strategic Tools for Customer Experience and
Operational Efficiency”
Ivanov and Webster (2021) explained that modern reservation systems have evolved into strategic
tools that improve customer experience and operational efficiency through automation and
analytics. The proposed system adopts this concept by implementing
automated scheduling and real-time calendar updates to prevent conflicts and improve booking
system because it supports the concept of integrating multiple services within a single online
platform. Similar to the system discussed in the study, the proposed platform will allow users to
browse entertainment services, view available packages, and book events through an online
interface. By centralizing these features, the system can enhance accessibility and improve service
coordination for both entertainers and clients.
“Web-Based Event Organizer Systems for Improved Business Process Integration and Event
Management Efficiency”
According to Lalo et al. (2025), web-based event organizer systems help improve business
the concepts discussed in the study, the proposed system will integrate booking management,
service packages, and scheduling tools in a single platform. By implementing these features, the
system can improve coordination between entertainers and clients while providing a more efficient
and organized event booking process.
“Web-Based Event Reservation Information Systems for Improved Booking and Scheduling
Efficiency”
According to Wahyuni et al. (2025), web-based event reservation information systems
significantly improve the efficiency of managing event bookings and scheduling processes. The
reservations, and obtain relevant information through digital platforms, which ultimately improves
customer satisfaction and operational efficiency.
Many researchers also pointed out that digital reservation systems help reduce common
operational issues such as scheduling conflicts, manual documentation errors, and inefficient
communication between service providers and clients. Features such as automated scheduling,
centralized data management, and real-time availability updates enable organizations to manage
bookings more accurately and effectively. These technologies also allow service providers to
monitor demand patterns, manage event schedules, and organize service offerings in a structured
with a convenient way to browse entertainment services, select service packages, and schedule
events while allowing entertainers to manage bookings and client information more efficient.
METHODOLOGY
Figure 1: Agile Methodology
The proponents used the Agile Development Methodology in developing the proposed Online Booking
and Scheduling Management System for Professional Event Entertainers Service. Agile was selected
because it is flexible, iterative, and suitable for a system with several modules such as client booking,
user management, payment verification, refund management, calendar scheduling, reschedule requests,
Agile, the proponents were able to monitor progress, adjust features based on system requirements, and
improve the overall functionality of the system.
The Agile methodology used in this project followed the steps of Planning, Designing, Development,
Testing, Review, and Deployment.
Planning Phase
The Planning Phase served as the foundation of the project. In this phase, the proponents identified the
main problems experienced by Aries the Magic Artist, especially the manual booking process, difficulty
in monitoring schedules, possible double booking, delayed updates, unorganized records, and manual
The proponents designed the user interface based on the role of each user. The Client interface was
designed for booking requests, package viewing, payment submission, refund requests, reschedule
requests, and notifications. The Owner interface was designed for payment verification, refund
verification, calendar control, reports, and business monitoring. The admin interface was designed for
booking records, user management, service and package management, calendar settings, and system
monitoring. The Staff interface was designed for monitoring bookings, schedules, reschedule requests,
completed events, and service-related tasks.
The database design was also planned during this phase. Important records such as users, roles,
Testing Phase
The Testing Phase was conducted to check whether the system features worked correctly and met the
intended requirements. The proponents tested the system by checking each module individually and then
testing how the modules worked together.
The proponents performed testing on the booking process, payment upload, Owner payment
verification, refund request handling, reschedule request approval, calendar monitoring, notifications,
reports, user login, and role-based access control. The system was also tested to check if users could
only access the modules assigned to their role.
Deployment Phase
The Deployment Phase involved preparing the system for actual use. After the main features were
developed and tested, the proponents prepared the system for deployment. This included checking the
database connection, application settings, file upload functionality, user roles, and final system behavior.
The system was prepared as a web-based application that can be accessed through internet browsers.
Since the system is intended to support online booking and scheduling, deployment allows users such as
Clients, Owners, Admins, and Staff to access the system based on their assigned roles.
In this phase, the proponents also checked whether the system could properly support the main business
Technical Background
The following technologies are utilized in the development of the system:
BACKEND DEVELOPMENT
• ASP.NET Core MVC (.NET 9) - is utilized as the main backend framework of the system.
It provides a structured approach in developing web applications by dividing the system
into three major components, namely the Model, View, and Controller. This framework is
used to manage the business logic of the application, process user requests, handle routing,
and connect the user interface with the database efficiently. It also supports the role-based
FRONTEND DEVELOPMENT
• Razor Views (.cshtml) - are utilized to create the dynamic user interface of the system.
They allow the combination of HTML and C# code in a single file, making it possible to
display data from the backend directly on web pages. This technology is used in rendering
the system pages such as booking forms, dashboard interfaces, calendar views, payment
modules, notification pages, and service management pages.
• HTML5 - is utilized as the standard markup language for structuring the web pages of the
system. It defines the layout and content of each page, including forms, tables, navigation
monitoring by displaying event dates, reservation counts, blocked dates, and booking
details in an organized and interactive interface.
ADDITIONAL FUNCTIONAL COMPONENTS
• File Upload using IFormFile - is utilized in the payment module of the system. It allows
clients to upload proof of downpayment images, which are then reviewed by the Owner
during payment verification. This supports the booking confirmation process by ensuring
that payment proof is recorded and checked before a booking is finalized.
• Email Sending Service is utilized through SMTP configuration and MailKit
CALENDAR OF ACTIVITIES
Figure 2. Gantt Chart
This section presents the detailed sequence of activities that the proponents will undergo in
completing the project. The development follows Agile methodology through structured sprints.
The project development was carried out using the Agile methodology through a series of
structured sprints. This approach enabled the proponents to divide the development process into
manageable phases, allowing continuous monitoring, evaluation, and improvement of the system.
Each sprint consisted of Sprint Planning, Development, Sprint Review, and Sprint Retrospective
Admin, and Staff, in order to define their specific roles and functions. In addition, the
database was designed using SQL Server, the overall system architecture was planned,
and the initial setup of the ASP.NET Core MVC project was completed to establish the
foundation for the succeeding development phases.
• Sprint 2 (Weeks 3–4): Core Module Development
In Sprint 2, conducted during Weeks 3 to 4, the proponents developed the core modules
of the system. The login and authentication features were implemented using ASP.NET
Core Identity to provide secure access to authorized users. Role-based access control
reports and visual summaries of system data. File upload functionality was also
developed to support the submission of proof of payment and other necessary
documents.
• SPRINT 5 (WEEKS 9–10): TESTING AND DEPLOYMENT
Finally, during Sprint 5, which took place in Weeks 9 to 10, the proponents
concentrated on testing, optimization, and deployment. Unit testing and integration
testing were conducted to verify the correctness and reliability of both individual
components and the system as a whole. Errors and performance issues identified during
deployment activities. These devices serve as the primary working environment for
developing the proposed web-based system.
• Stable Internet Connection – is utilized to support online development activities,
package installation, cloud deployment, email configuration, testing of online
functionalities, and access to external development resources. It also ensures proper
communication between the system and cloud-based services.
• Cloud Hosting Services – are utilized to deploy and host the web application online.
This allows the proposed system to become accessible through internet browsers and
• ASP.NET Core Identity – is utilized for authentication and role-based authorization.
It manages user registration, login, password recovery, and role restrictions for Admin,
Staff, and Client accounts.
• Visual Studio 2022 – is utilized as the primary Integrated Development Environment
(IDE) for developing, debugging, testing, and maintaining the proposed system.
• Visual Studio Code – is utilized as an additional lightweight code editor for frontend
customization, quick modifications, and interface styling.
• Bootstrap 5 – is utilized as the frontend CSS framework for creating responsive,
Requirements Analysis
In this part, the proposed system provides computing solutions to address the needs of event-based
entertainment service providers in terms of the following:
• WHO
o Clients – The clients are individuals who book entertainment services for their
events through the proposed system. They can view packages, submit booking
requests, upload proof of payment, request rescheduling, request refunds, and
monitor transaction status.
o Refund Management – The system allows clients to submit refund requests
and allows the Owner to approve or reject refund requests based on booking
and payment records.
o Package Management – The system allows the admin to manage service
packages, prices, inclusions, and package availability.
o Reports and Analytics – The system allows the Owner to view booking,
payment, income, and package trend reports for business monitoring and
decision-making.
Requirements Documentation
This section establishes the basis of agreement between the client and the developers regarding the
system’s functionalities.
Under Agile methodology, requirements are documented through:
• Product Backlog – A complete list of system features
• SPRINT BACKLOG – TASKS SELECTED FOR EACH SPRINT
• User Stories – Functional descriptions of system requirements
Examples of User Stories:
• As a staff member, I want to view completed bookings so that past event records remain
organized.
All software features are enumerated and implemented incrementally per sprint. System interfaces
are presented through designed Razor Views (.cshtml) showing how the software appears once
developed and deployed.

The system follows a layered architectural pattern:
1. Presentation Layer – Razor Views (.cshtml), HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap
2. Business Logic Layer – ASP.NET Core MVC Controllers and Services
3. Data Access Layer – Entity Framework Core
4. Database Layer – SQL Server
Security is implemented using ASP.NET Core Identity with role-based authentication and
authorization for Client, Owner, Admin, and Staff users. Each role has different access permissions
based on its responsibilities. Clients can submit and monitor transactions, Owners can verify
